title: Public.com Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Public.com Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Public.com (4.0★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Public.com — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Public.com?

Public.com is a commission-free social investing platform founded in 2019 by Public Holdings, Inc. and based in New York. It lets you trade stocks, ETFs, options, crypto, and bonds without paying commissions, while connecting with a built-in community of investors and accessing AI-powered stock analysis. With a 4.0/5 rating, it combines the execution simplicity of a modern broker with learning tools and social features designed to help beginner and intermediate investors build confidence. Public.com offers a free tier and an optional $10/month premium plan, making it accessible whether you're just starting out or gradually scaling your portfolio.

How to Create Your Public.com Account

Opening a Public.com account takes about 10–15 minutes and requires only basic information. Here's the process:

  • Download the app or visit public.com — Public.com is mobile-first, so you'll get the best experience on iPhone or Android, though you can also sign up on the web.
  • Provide your email and create a password — Start with a strong password you haven't used elsewhere.
  • Enter personal information — Name, date of birth, address, phone number, and employment status. You'll also need your Social Security number (SSN) for tax identification.
  • Verify your identity — Public.com uses digital identity verification. You'll take a photo of your ID (driver's license or passport) and may need to complete a brief video verification. This typically takes 1–2 minutes.
  • Link your bank account — Add your checking or savings account for deposits and withdrawals. Public.com uses Plaid for secure bank linking; you'll provide your online banking credentials once, and they verify with a small test deposit or instant verification depending on your bank.
  • Review and accept agreements — Sign the customer agreement, margin agreement (if applying for options or margin), and acknowledge the Risk Disclosure.
  • Fund your account — Once approved (usually instant), transfer money from your bank. The first transfer can take 1–2 business days; subsequent transfers are often faster.

After approval, your account is live and you can start trading immediately. If you want options trading, you may need to apply for options approval, which is typically granted on the same day for most applicants.

Setting Up Public.com for the First Time

Once you're funded, take 10 minutes to configure your account for a smoother experience:

  • Customize your dashboard — The home screen shows your portfolio, price alerts, and a feed of community posts. Reorder the widgets to show what matters most to you: your watchlist, portfolio performance, or trending stocks in the community.
  • Set up price alerts — Go to any stock or asset detail page and tap the bell icon to set alerts at specific prices. You'll get push notifications (on mobile) or in-app alerts when triggered. This is useful for entry/exit targets without needing to watch all day.
  • Configure notification preferences — In Settings > Notifications, choose which alerts you want (price alerts, news, social activity from followed investors). Disable noisy categories if needed.
  • Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) — Under Settings > Security, turn on 2FA using an authenticator app or SMS. This protects your account from unauthorized access, especially important if you're trading frequently.
  • Review cash account settings — Public.com offers a High-Yield Cash Account integrated with the brokerage. Go to Settings > Account and check your cash sweep rate if you hold uninvested cash.
  • Connect to your watchlist — Search for stocks or ETFs you're interested in and tap the heart icon to add them to your watchlist. You'll see price changes, news, and Alpha AI insights on each one.

Essential Features You Should Know

Alpha AI Analysis — Public.com's Alpha AI tool generates plain-language stock analysis for any holding in plain English, not jargon. Tap the Alpha icon on any stock detail page to see AI-generated insights on fundamentals, technicals, and sentiment. It's designed to demystify stock research for beginners, and it updates continuously.

Mobile App and Dashboard — The app is the primary interface and it's polished. You get real-time quotes, instant order execution, and all trading features from your phone. The web interface exists but is secondary; treat the app as your main trading terminal.

Social Features and Community Feed — The community feed shows what other Public.com investors are buying, selling, and discussing. You can follow other investors, see their portfolios and trades, comment on positions, and learn from their rationale. This is not paper trading; all trades are real, so you're watching actual money move. Use this as a learning tool, not a signal to blindly copy.

News Feed and Real-Time Updates — Integrated news for every stock you're watching or holding. Swipe to the News tab on any stock to see recent company news, SEC filings, earnings announcements, and market commentary. This keeps you informed without jumping between apps.

Performance Analytics — Your portfolio page breaks down gains/losses by asset type, shows your all-time return, and tracks your win rate (percentage of winning trades). There's also a performance chart you can toggle between different time periods. However, note that Public.com lacks desktop charting tools for detailed technical analysis; for that, you'd use external tools like TradingView.

Options Trading at $0 Per Contract — Options cost $0 per contract (no standard $0.65 per-contract fee), making it cheap to test options strategies. You can trade calls, puts, spreads, and other strategies. Apply for options approval in Settings > Permissions after opening your account.

Education and Learning Resources — Public.com provides educational articles, glossaries, and explainers on the platform. Access these from the Learn section in the app. They cover basics like what stocks are, how to read a balance sheet, and options strategies for beginners.

Public.com Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Free Tier ($0/month) — Access to all trading (stocks, ETFs, options, crypto, bonds), real-time quotes, basic performance analytics, the community feed, and AI analysis. No hidden fees; no commission on any trade. This is genuinely free and fully functional. Best for beginners, buy-and-hold investors, and anyone who doesn't need premium extras.

Premium Plan ($10/month) — Includes everything in the free tier plus advanced features: priority customer support, exclusive market research reports, premium educational content, and early access to new features. The premium edge is subtle; most casual and intermediate investors never feel they need it. Only upgrade if you're actively trading multiple times a week and want faster support.

Recommendation by trader type:

  • Beginners: Use the free tier. Everything you need to learn and start investing is included. There's no cost barrier to experimentation.
  • Intermediate traders: Start free. If you're trading options or multi-leg strategies frequently and want research reports, upgrade to Premium. Otherwise, free is sufficient.
  • Active traders or professionals: Public.com isn't optimized for you. No backtesting, no advanced charting, no futures. Consider Fidelity or Interactive Brokers instead.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Public.com

  • Use Alpha AI before buying, not after. Tap Alpha on a stock you're considering. It'll show you consensus on fundamentals and sentiment. Don't use it to validate a trade you've already decided on; use it to challenge your thinking first.
  • Set price alerts for your entry and exit targets, not news events. Public.com's alerts work best for technical levels. Set a $5 alert on a stock you want to buy at $50, then mute notifications. You'll be alerted when price hits your target, not distracted by every earnings announcement.
  • Follow experienced investors in your interest area, not just the most-followed. Search by company (e.g., search "Tesla" and sort by followers) to find investors actively trading in sectors you care about. Their trades and rationale teach more than general market commentary.
  • Use the community feed to stress-test your thesis, not to find new ideas. Before entering a large position, scroll the feed and see what others are saying. If everyone's bearish and you're bullish, make sure you have a good reason. Consensus is often wrong, but ignoring it entirely is reckless.
  • Keep uninvested cash in the High-Yield Cash Account. Public.com's cash sweep pays competitive yields (rates vary). If you're holding cash waiting for an entry point, don't leave it earning 0.01% in a money-market fund.
  • Paperless your confirmations and tax documents. Go to Settings > Tax and enable e-statements. Public.com emails Form 1099-B, 1099-DIV, and 1099-INT automatically each January for tax filing. No paper clutter.
  • Test options with small positions first. $0 per-contract fees make it tempting to over-trade options. Paper trade mentally by watching positions before pulling the trigger, or start with single contracts to learn mechanics before spreading larger capital.

Common Public.com Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue: Your identity verification is rejected or stuck in pending. Fix: This usually means your ID photo didn't scan clearly or your face didn't match your ID photo clearly. Log back into the app, go to Settings > Verification, and retake the photos in good lighting with your full face visible and ID fully in frame. If it's still stuck after 24 hours, contact support via in-app chat.

Issue: Bank transfer is delayed or rejected. Fix: Most delays happen on the first transfer (1–2 business days is normal). If it fails, your bank may have rejected it due to name mismatch or fraud filters. Check your bank's transaction log; if it shows a pending debit, it will likely clear. If rejected, Public.com will show an error. Try again with a smaller amount, or contact your bank to ask why it was declined and authorize Public.com's partner for ACH transfers.

Issue: You can't find advanced charting or technical analysis tools. Fix: They don't exist on Public.com. The platform intentionally keeps charting simple for beginners. Use TradingView (free or paid) alongside Public.com if you need candlestick patterns, moving averages, or indicators. Link your Public.com and TradingView watchlists manually or via browser bookmarks to switch between them.

Issue: Your options approval is taking longer than expected. Fix: Options approval usually takes minutes to a few hours. If it's been more than 24 hours, check Settings > Permissions to see if you're pending review. If status shows "pending," contact support. Public.com prioritizes approval during business hours; applying on Friday evening may delay until Monday.

Is Public.com Worth It? Our Verdict

Public.com is worth opening for beginners and intermediate buy-and-hold investors. The free tier delivers all essential trading tools, zero commissions, $0 per-contract options, and a supportive community that makes learning feel collaborative instead of lonely. The social angle is its defining feature — you're not just trading in isolation; you're trading alongside thousands of other investors, seeing their moves, and building conviction through dialogue. If you're new to investing or building a core long-term portfolio, this is one of the easiest on-ramps available in 2026. However, if you're an active trader, options professional, or futures trader needing advanced charting and backtesting, look at Public.com vs Robinhood for a closer feature comparison, or move to Interactive Brokers for institutional-grade tools. For the beginner or casual investor, Public.com's 4.0/5 rating is earned.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
